"I bought Nightclub Simulator for $6.99, but the game is still showing that I’m on the trial version. I have already paid for the full game. Is anyone else having this same problem? How can I fix this?"

developer_response.body: You need to completely delete the previous version and then download it again.

Last time i played it (it was 3 months ago) it was ngl pretty shi but now its peak like the first time i played it i mean the graphics are bad but i will not complain about it because i focus more on gameplay then on graphics. The only downside of this game is the paid multiplayer and updates are mainly for it (most of the people are giving 1 star bc of bad graphics and multiplayer glaze) but the game is very good and if you want a fun streetfight like game and dont care about graphics, paid multiplayer this game is for you.
Overall i just recommend it.

multiplayer should've been free. I understand why its not, because obviously the developers have spent a lot of hard work and time into making it, and thats understandable, so ill let it slide. I have a few ideas, starting with: 
● turning off the bouncers/guards
they are so annoying when you wanna start a fight and they just get in the way, so a toggle like the police would be a good option, rather than me having to turn on breaking bones just to off them in a corner.
●accessories? 
things like hats and such
●utilise the second floor in the club!

Ideas or updates this game should add is Avatar Mods for creativity and entertainment, next thing is to add more guns and grenades for more methods to kill other players or npcs, and the last thing is to polish your multiplayer gamemode adding better mechanics and physics.

I know yall are probably having similar thinkings like what I mentioned but it’s just some ideas that I would like to see in the future for this game. Otherwise this game is pretty fun.

Since my original review giving it 4 stars, I'm now giving it 5 because I find I play it quite regular and it took a lot of hours before it sometimes gets a bit boring.

However overall in short bursts its both fun and great stress relief, the ability to download a lot of mods is also very easy and convenient.

One thing that isn't changed since my initial review is, It is quite frustrating for example when you hit a guard with an axe and it does like 4 damage or less when they have over 100 hp, it just feels very unsatisfying.

I wish they gave you the options in the mod/stats menu to change your own stats including hp etc, it baffle me that they haven't.

This game removes my stress, i love the blood feature and i love how you can get mods. The only thing is buying multiplayer even though we bought the game but without is alot of fun. I recommend this amazing game

reviews.review_title: Read this if you want an honest opinion.
reviews.review_description:
This game is alright, but NEEDS some fixes. Here is what I think:

1. Simply bad graphics. Looks like a unity game. NPC's look stolen, guns absolutely SUCK, and weapons glitch.

2. Way too focused on multiplayer. I get it, you dropped it somewhat recently, but 3 DOLLARS for multiplayer!? It should come FREE with the game. Again, I do understand why they did this. The game is cheap and pretty dead. Multiplayer has different things than singleplayer, which reminds me-

3. Multiplayer exclusive stuff, like a Model 870, A DB Shotgun, A UMP-45, and a better glock. And multiplayer has some tweaks, like different models, different maps, etc.

4: "Gore" sucks: Again, I understand that a fair amount of kids play. But the disgusting part? You simply allow alcohol, STRIPPERS (which Ik there's a button to disable, but still.) "front and back" (due to rules, I cannot specifically say, but you know.) grabbing spots. Yet, All I get for "gore" is blood. 

5: MOST IMPORTANT ONE!!! PLEASE READ: WAYYYY too depending on mods. The game ITSELF uses maps from mods in the gallery. 

All and All, the game is BAD. Heavily recommend BONELAB. Thank you for reading.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave such detailed feedback. We appreciate players sharing their honest opinions, and we do take these comments seriously.

A few things we’d like to clarify about Nightclub Simulator:

Graphics
Nightclub Simulator is built as a sandbox-style experience, which means a lot of the development focus has gone into interactions, systems, and gameplay freedom rather than purely visual fidelity. That said, we are constantly working on improving models, environments, and fixing bugs as the game continues to evolve.

Multiplayer
Multiplayer was added later in development and requires servers, networking, and ongoing support, which is why it is offered separately. We wanted to keep the base game affordable while still allowing players who want the multiplayer experience to access it.

Multiplayer Content
Some features and content appear in multiplayer to give that mode its own gameplay style and variety. As development continues, we are always evaluating balance and what content should be shared across modes.

Content and Settings
Nightclub Simulator includes optional settings that allow players to adjust certain content, including turning features on or off. The goal is to give players control over their experience. The gore setting has been turned down due to meta guidelines. 

Mods
The game is designed as a sandbox, and sandbox games often benefit from community mods. Mods allow players to create their own environments, props, and experiences, which helps keep the game fresh and gives the community a way to expand the game beyond the base content.

We know the game isn’t perfect and we are continuously working on updates and improvements. Feedback like yours helps us understand what players want to see improved in future updates.
